purpose: Adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing queuing commands after the device has been unregistered and avoiding a use‑after‑free.
Removes an unnecessary asm/termios.h include from n_hdlc.c, cleaning up header dependencies.
Updates the perf c2c documentation to reflect the addition of the 'N/A' metric for store operations in the report table.
Fixes race and double‑free bugs in hugetlb VMA lock handling during split and unmap, ensures locks exist for all VM_MAYSHARE VMA, and corrects pmd sharing logic.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply definitions to the Realtek rt5682s device tree binding, documenting the codec's power supply requirements.
Adds missing iounmap() in zorro7xx_remove_one() to free memory mapped by ioremap() during probe error handling, eliminating a resource leak.
Removes an unused 'work' field from the mlx5e_async_ctx structure, cleaning up the code.
Change cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed to allow -1 value and silence static checker warnings when key is NULL.
The commit reorganizes the order of CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS entries in RISC-V defconfig files to match the sorting used by savedefconfig, without altering functionality.
Adds a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace inline checks for CAN‑FD mode and updates code to use it.
Adds logic to selftests for RTC to skip tests when no RTC device is present, avoiding false failures.
Removes a dead store to variable 'bytes' in ALSA echoaudio midi.c, eliminating a redundant assignment that was never read and clearing a clang warning.
Decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, removing unnecessary RCU Tasks build when rcuscale is enabled, thus reducing kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase "be split" from the KVM x86 MMU header spte.h, cleaning up a comment or macro definition.
Reverts a change that populated the dcn315 clock table based on dcfclk, which caused an underflow when dcfclk states were disabled, thereby fixing that bug.
Adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali‑G31 GPU in the device tree bindings.
Renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp to align naming with other DW PCIe core driver entities, improving consistency.
Fixes a format string mismatch in amd-xgbe driver, changing %hx to %x to match promoted int argument, eliminating clang -Wformat warning.
Fixes a branch range check bug in MIPS jump_label by casting upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing negative offset warnings.

The commit removes an unused case from the SetHwReg8188EU() switch that merely forwarded to a function, replacing it with a direct function call. This simplifies the HAL layer and eliminates redundant code.
Makes previously non-static symbols in cpu_errata.c static to satisfy sparse, removing warnings and limiting scope.

The commit removes an incorrect interrupt flags cell from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings, correcting a misconfiguration that caused failures when consumers specified flags for a single-cell provider.
Adds dynamic allocation of array space in struct ctrl_ref to support variable array sizes set by drivers, enabling new control array handling in V4L2 media subsystem.
Adds support for additional Qualcomm SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver, expanding hardware recognition.
Adds support for all 16 chip cuts (A–P) in rtl8xxxu driver, replacing the previous 'unknown' output for cuts beyond E.
Removes the .is_enabled field from PRCMU clock structures, relying on the core's existing software enable count.
Refactors RISC-V memory init code to reduce #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage by grouping related macros and moving pt_ops macro, improving maintainability.
Adds device tree entries to enable QUP and GPI DMA on the SM6350-Lena platform, allowing future I2C nodes to function.
Updates comments and coding style in thermal driver rzg2l, correcting wording, typo, and comment formatting.

The commit changes the release_swfw_sync routine to avoid an infinite loop when the hardware semaphore cannot be acquired, typically after a surprise hardware removal. It logs an error and exits instead of looping.
Updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace legacy bus clock defines with modern DAI format terminology, improving code clarity and consistency.
Removes an unnecessary IS_SWAPFILE macro/hack from NFS code, simplifying logic and eliminating dead code.
Adds handling for GTP echo requests by sending echo responses with required recovery information, ensuring compliance with GTP specifications.
Replaces cpumask_next_* calls with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, adding a cpumask_first_zero() helper to simplify zero-bit searches and improve performance.
Removes the unsupported dtim_assoc IOVAR call from the brcmfmac driver, eliminating repeated firmware error messages after STA roaming.
Adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the Intel RT5682 codec to support headset codecs without a speaker amplifier, connecting the codec to SSP0 per Intel BT offload design.
Adds a check to io_uring net sendmsg path that fails zero-copy sendmsg operations when the underlying socket protocol does not support zerocopy, mirroring behavior already added for send requests.

Adds a check for xeCJK.sty before loading it in the kernel documentation preamble, preventing errors when the xeCJK package is missing or broken. This fixes a known bug and also guards against future failures.
Adjusts the checkpatch script to suppress false positives for macros that use 'case' statements without parentheses, allowing existing kernel drivers to pass linting.
Replaces simple-KMS helper functions in the simple DRM driver with the standard atomic helpers, improving architecture and code sharing. No functional changes; purely a refactor.
Adds a JPEG decoding worker interface to the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver, enabling three hardware decoders to run concurrently on MT8195, improving parallel decoding capability.
Removes VPRINTK() debug prints and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper, relying on tracepoints for logging.
Adds logic to enable AMD display driver to enter zstate during extended vblank when in FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO, calculating extra time and writing registers.
Updates the hqd_destroy() callback prototype to use uint32_t for reset_type, matching actual implementations and eliminating a CFI failure.
Corrects the property name in STM32 pinctrl bindings from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to the binding schema, fixing a bug and preventing future misconfigurations.
Suppresses a spurious GCC 12 array-bounds warning in ath9k_hw_resettxqueue by lowering the warning level, without changing functionality.
Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver to use the more appropriate runtime PM API, simplifying the code path.
Moves fixed file install/remove logic into filetable code, changes helper signatures to use ctx instead of io_kiocb, improving code organization.
Adds separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes to the i.MX8MQ VPU device tree bindings, enabling independent power-domain control via vpu-blk-ctrl driver.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro, which was only used once inside IWL_DEVICE_BZ, and inlines its contents into that macro, simplifying the code base.
